title: Broadcast rate limiting
description:
## Why

Subscribed clients can currently broadcast unlimited messages. There is no rate limit on broadcast, sealed_broadcast, or group_send. A single compromised or malicious client can flood the relay and degrade service for all other users.

## What

Add an in-process per-pub_id rate limiter in AppState:

- Data structure: `rate_limits: DashMap<String, (u32, Instant)>` keyed by pub_id
- Window: 60 seconds, max 120 broadcasts per window
- Configuration: `RATE_LIMIT_MSG_PER_MIN` env var (default 120)
- On exceed: return JSON-RPC error { code: -32020, message: 'RATE_LIMITED' }
- Applies to: broadcast, sealed_broadcast, group_send
- Exempt: whisper (1:1 direct delivery has natural cost), all non-send methods
- Counters: in-memory only, no persistence — counters reset on relay restart

## Acceptance Criteria

- A client sending >120 broadcasts in 60s receives error -32020 after the 120th
- Whisper messages are not rate-limited
- RATE_LIMIT_MSG_PER_MIN env var changes the limit without code change
- Unit test covers: under-limit passes, at-limit passes, over-limit rejected, window reset unblocks
- DESIGN.md §Rate Limiting documents error code -32020 as a stable client-facing contract
close_reason: Broadcast rate limiting implemented: rolling 60s window, DashMap per-pub_id counters, RATE_LIMITED -32020, configurable via RATE_LIMIT_MSG_PER_MIN (default 120). Wired into BROADCAST, SEALED_BROADCAST, GROUP_SEND. Cleanup on disconnect. 5 unit tests. DESIGN.md updated.
